title: Feature-Market Fit — NAICS 334413 Semiconductor and Related Device Manufacturing

sections.title: Step 09 — Technology-Scope Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
Six technology-scope features from `01_product_decomposition.md` scored against the single market (334413). Rationale grounded in top JTBD needs: N05 cert delay (opp 17), N08 ride-through proof (16), N10 wafer-scrap (16), N11 revenue-stack vs ride-through (16), N12 ≤16 ms transition (16), N13 fire (15), N03 MV feeder (14), N06 parallel cert (14), N18 Netzentgelte+decarb (13).

### 09a — Per-market scoring (1 market × 6 features)

| # | Feature | Kano | Time | Cost | Safety | Reliab | Skill | Stress | Pain | Conf | Delight | overall_fit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration | `must_be` | 3 | 6 | 2 | 5 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 4 | 3 | **3.56** |
| F2 | Bidirectional Power Conversion System | `one_dimensional` | 6 | 7 | 5 | 7 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 6 | 4 | **5.22** |
| F3 | Medium-Voltage Grid Coupling | `must_be` | 3 | 5 | 5 | 7 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 5 | 2 | **4.11** |
| F4 | Energy Management System (EMS) | `attractive` | 5 | 8 | 4 | 6 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 6 | 7 | **5.67** |
| F5 | Grid-Code Certification Delivery | `must_be` | 8 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 7 | 8 | 6 | 7 | 3 | **6.33** |
| F6 | Civil Works & Site Preparation | `must_be` | 3 | 3 | 6 | 4 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | **3.22** |

### Kano rationale per feature

| Feature | Why this class |
|---|---|
| F1 | Containerized Li-ion is the **baseline of the commodity** (must_be). For fabs the BESS enables A4 but **creates** the fire-risk (N13) that rotating-mass A1/flywheel alternatives do not carry — score muted on Safety (2) and Reliability (5) because Li-ion is the weakest alternative on SEMI F47 sub-200 ms proof (N12). Incumbents Piller/Hitec set the ride-through bar. |
| F2 | Bidirectional PCS is **one_dimensional** — faster response time + more MW = more revenue-stack headroom and tighter ride-through. Primary drivers: Reliability 7 (enables N12), Cost 7 (enables N18 revenue stack), Confidence 6. Not must_be because dynamic-UPS (A1) and DVR (A3) deliver ride-through without a 4-quadrant PCS. |
| F3 | MV coupling is `must_be` for grid-scale assets but the incumbent S1 installation is LV tool-level (Schneider Galaxy VXL, Piller UNIBLOCK) — so MV coupling is **overscoped** for the fab's tool-protection need. Reliability 7 from MV-side hardening; Delight 2 because MV touches DSO interface that fab utilities already own. |
| F4 | EMS is the **attractive** feature in this market: unlocks N18 Netzentgelte+decarb board scorecard (the CFO hook) and N11 revenue-stack arbitration. Drivers: Cost 8, Delight 7, Confidence 6. Incumbents Piller/Eaton have no revenue-stack EMS — pure ride-through boxes. Fabs do not expect this capability from a UPS-class vendor. |
| F5 | Grid-code certification delivery is `must_be` for MV-scale, **but** the fab's dominant certification concern is **SEMI F47** (see N08), not VDE-AR-N 4110. Time 8 and Stress 8 are highest of any feature because N05 (certification delay pushing COD) is the #1 opportunity (17). However, Mein Solar's VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 path does **not** include SEMI F47 qualification. Confidence 7 because EPC-led cert discipline translates; Delight 3 because fabs take it for granted. |
| F6 | Civil works is `must_be` baseline for any L1 EPC offering. Low scores because fab GCs (M+W, Exyte, Jacobs) already own civil scope — Mein Solar's civil capability is duplicative on S4 greenfield. Safety 6 only because fire-clearance foundation detailing is safety-adjacent. |

### 09b — Cross-market validation checks (self-applied)

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Kano distribution reasonable (not all must_be) | PASS — 4 must_be, 1 one_dimensional, 1 attractive |
| overall_fit arithmetic | PASS — all equal mean of 9 scores rounded to 2 dp |
| No feature scores 10 on all dimensions | PASS |
| safety ≥ 7 but confidence ≤ 3 | PASS — no such pair |
| skill ≥ 7 but stress ≤ 3 | PASS — F5 (skill 7, stress 8) consistent |
| Feature names comply with 1.pdf rules | PASS — all 2–6 words, noun phrases, no brand/outcome/superlative |
| Reliability-low on measurement-accuracy feature flag | N/A — no measurement feature in scope |
| Safety-low on safety-interlock feature flag | N/A |

### Top-fit verdict

**F5 Grid-Code Certification Delivery (6.33)** and **F4 EMS (5.67)** are the strongest fits because they map directly to the top two opportunity needs for the fab buyer: certification-path competence (N05, N06) and revenue-stack quantification (N18, N11). **F1 Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration (3.56)** is the weakest — not because the feature is unimportant, but because the fab market discounts it heavily on Safety and Reliability vs. rotating-mass incumbents.

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
Litmus: "Would this need disappear if the fab chose **dynamic UPS / flywheel (A1)**, **static battery UPS at tool level (A2)**, or **active voltage conditioner (A3)** instead of the Mein Solar BESS EPC (A4)?" If YES → include. If NO → already a market need in Step 06.

### 10a — Generated product-related needs

| ID | Statement | Source constraint | Affected mkts | Imp | Sat | Opp |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P01 | Minimize the likelihood of a high-energy-density Li-ion thermal runaway event triggering >24 h fab-wide evacuation | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | 334413 | 10 | 5 | 15 |
| P02 | Minimize the likelihood of fire-clearance zoning adding >15 m setback that forces BESS siting outside the fab security perimeter | Fire-Protection Clearance | 334413 | 8 | 4 | 12 |
| P03 | Minimize the likelihood of 10–15% round-trip energy losses eroding >15% of projected Netzentgelte peak-shaving and EPEX arbitrage revenue | Round-Trip Energy Losses | 334413 | 7 | 4 | 10 |
| P04 | Minimize the likelihood of ambient-extreme HVAC duty cycling reducing contracted ride-through autonomy by >5% within 24 months | Ambient Temperature Envelope | 334413 | 7 | 4 | 10 |
| P05 | Minimize the likelihood of LFP cell supply disruption (CN concentration, EU Battery Reg compliance) adding >12 weeks to fab-ramp-critical COD | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | 334413 | 8 | 3 | 13 |
| P06 | Minimize the likelihood of installed cost exceeding €400/kWh such that CFO capex board rejects the project despite revenue-stack model | CAPEX Viability Threshold | 334413 | 9 | 3 | 15 |
| P07 | Minimize the likelihood of container footprint (40–80 m²/MWh) + fire-service access requiring >20% of planned fab utility-yard area | Land Footprint and Siting | 334413 | 7 | 4 | 10 |
| P08 | Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack dispatch state of charge compromising the ≤16 ms source-transition window during a grid disturbance | Round-Trip Energy Losses (dispatch coupling) | 334413 | 9 | 2 | 16 |

**Generation count:** 8 product-related needs from 6 distinct constraints. P08 is dual-sourced (round-trip coupling to dispatch state) and overlaps in spirit with JTBD N11 — kept because the Step 06 market need is solution-agnostic whereas P08 names the Li-ion dispatch-SoC mechanism.

### 10b — Validation checks

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Litmus: would need disappear with dynamic-UPS / DVR / flywheel? | PASS — flywheel has no fire (P01), no Li-ion supply (P05), no CAPEX-per-kWh (P06), sub-cycle DVR has no m²/MWh (P07), no 10–15% RTE (P03/P08) |
| Source constraint traceability | PASS — each `source_constraint` maps 1:1 to a Step 01 constraint |
| Affected-markets plausibility | PASS — only 334413 in scope for this JTBD invocation |
| Statement includes causal mechanism | PASS — "thermal runaway", "10–15% round-trip", "CN concentration + EU Battery Reg", "40–80 m²/MWh + fire-service", "dispatch state of charge" |
| Duplication with Step 06 market needs | PASS — N13 (fire) and P01 overlap but P01 names the **Li-ion-specific** causal mechanism while N13 is solution-agnostic; retained per JTBD framework |
| Importance<3 AND satisfaction>7 (low-value) | PASS — none |
| Completeness: every constraint generates ≥1 need | 6 of 9 constraints generate needs. MV Grid Connection Capacity + Grid Code Cert Lead Time are **market needs** (apply to any MV-connected storage EPC regardless of chemistry). Land Footprint generates P02 and P07. |

### 10c — Constraints that did NOT generate a product-related need (and why)

| Constraint | Reason not product-related |
|---|---|
| MV Grid Connection Capacity | Affects **any** grid-connected capital asset (commodity-level); captured as market need (JTBD N03) |
| Grid Code Certification Lead Time | Applies to any MV-connected storage EPC; market-level (JTBD N05, N06) |
| Land Footprint and Siting | Two needs generated (P02, P07); partial overlap with market need on siting — retained because m²/MWh is chemistry-dependent |

---

sections.title: Integration with JTBD needs (334413)
sections.content:
Cross-reference of Step 10 product-related needs with JTBD error statements:

| Step 10 | JTBD analog | Relationship |
|---|---|---|
| P01 fire/evacuation | N13 (imp 10, sat 5, opp 15, product_related=true) | **SAME** — P01 names the causal mechanism, N13 is the solution-agnostic market bridge |
| P08 dispatch SoC vs 16 ms transition | N11 (imp 9, sat 2, opp 16) + N12 (imp 10, sat 4, opp 16) | **COUPLED** — P08 is the product-related root cause of both N11 and N12 |
| P04 autonomy degradation | N14 (imp 8, sat 4, opp 12) | **SAME** — P04 names Li-ion calendar-aging causal mechanism |
| P06 CAPEX CFO rejection | N18 (imp 8, sat 3, opp 13, Purchase Executor hook) | **COUPLED** — CAPEX threshold is the denominator of the CFO business case |

---

sections.title: Strategic Read
sections.content:
| Insight | Evidence |
|---|---|
| **F5 + F4 are Mein Solar's real wedge in 334413** (fit 6.33 + 5.67) | Certification-path discipline (N05 opp 17) + Netzentgelte+decarb scorecard (N18 opp 13) — neither dynamic UPS nor DVR incumbents deliver EMS revenue-stack proof |
| **F1 is the weak link** (fit 3.56) | Li-ion introduces fire risk (P01, N13) and lags sub-200 ms proof (N08) vs. Piller dynamic UPS — must be mitigated with engineered fire envelope + SEMI F47 qualification, not marketing |
| **P08 is the dealkiller** | The single product-related need (opp 16, sat 2) where Li-ion BESS structurally conflicts with fab ride-through requirement — revenue-stack dispatch SoC vs. sub-200 ms transition |
| **P06 gates entry** | CAPEX viability at €250–400/kWh must be defended with subsidy stack (KfW, innovation tenders) or lost to DVR+battery-hybrid alternatives at lower €/kWh |

Consistent with JTBD strategic read: **MEDIUM priority — defer until 1–2 reference installations land in HIGH-priority markets** (standalone BESS IPP 221118, solar+BESS 221114, C&I landlords 531120). In 334413 enter as **subcontractor to fab GCs on S4 greenfield / S5 retrofit**, leading with PV+BESS decarbonisation (N18) and letting ride-through qualification (N08) mature.

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
Upstream files only:
- `01_product_decomposition.md` — features, constraints, commodity FP
- `02_functional_promise.md` — commodity-level FP anchor for litmus test
- `05_market_discovery.md` — market identity (NAICS 334413, distance 6, MEDIUM priority)
- `JTBD_semiconductor_and_related_device_manufac.md` — error statements N01–N18, incumbent alternatives A1–A5, strategic read

rows.Why this class: Containerized Li-ion is the **baseline of the commodity** (must_be). For fabs the BESS enables A4 but **creates** the fire-risk (N13) that rotating-mass A1/flywheel alternatives do not carry — score muted on Safety (2) and Reliability (5) because Li-ion is the weakest alternative on SEMI F47 sub-200 ms proof (N12). Incumbents Piller/Hitec set the ride-through bar.

Bidirectional PCS is **one_dimensional** — faster response time + more MW = more revenue-stack headroom and tighter ride-through. Primary drivers: Reliability 7 (enables N12), Cost 7 (enables N18 revenue stack), Confidence 6. Not must_be because dynamic-UPS (A1) and DVR (A3) deliver ride-through without a 4-quadrant PCS.

MV coupling is `must_be` for grid-scale assets but the incumbent S1 installation is LV tool-level (Schneider Galaxy VXL, Piller UNIBLOCK) — so MV coupling is **overscoped** for the fab's tool-protection need. Reliability 7 from MV-side hardening; Delight 2 because MV touches DSO interface that fab utilities already own.

EMS is the **attractive** feature in this market: unlocks N18 Netzentgelte+decarb board scorecard (the CFO hook) and N11 revenue-stack arbitration. Drivers: Cost 8, Delight 7, Confidence 6. Incumbents Piller/Eaton have no revenue-stack EMS — pure ride-through boxes. Fabs do not expect this capability from a UPS-class vendor.

Grid-code certification delivery is `must_be` for MV-scale, **but** the fab's dominant certification concern is **SEMI F47** (see N08), not VDE-AR-N 4110. Time 8 and Stress 8 are highest of any feature because N05 (certification delay pushing COD) is the #1 opportunity (17). However, Mein Solar's VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 path does **not** include SEMI F47 qualification. Confidence 7 because EPC-led cert discipline translates; Delight 3 because fabs take it for granted.

Civil works is `must_be` baseline for any L1 EPC offering. Low scores because fab GCs (M+W, Exyte, Jacobs) already own civil scope — Mein Solar's civil capability is duplicative on S4 greenfield. Safety 6 only because fire-clearance foundation detailing is safety-adjacent.

rows.Status: PASS — all equal mean of 9 scores rounded to 2 dp

rows.Check: No feature scores 10 on all dimensions

rows.Evidence: Certification-path discipline (N05 opp 17) + Netzentgelte+decarb scorecard (N18 opp 13) — neither dynamic UPS nor DVR incumbents deliver EMS revenue-stack proof
Li-ion introduces fire risk (P01, N13) and lags sub-200 ms proof (N08) vs. Piller dynamic UPS — must be mitigated with engineered fire envelope + SEMI F47 qualification, not marketing
The single product-related need (opp 16, sat 2) where Li-ion BESS structurally conflicts with fab ride-through requirement — revenue-stack dispatch SoC vs. sub-200 ms transition
CAPEX viability at €250–400/kWh must be defended with subsidy stack (KfW, innovation tenders) or lost to DVR+battery-hybrid alternatives at lower €/kWh

entities.technology_class: Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC

entries.rationale: Containerized Li-ion is commodity baseline (must_be) but in 334413 the feature underscores the exact risk the fab least wants: thermal-runaway (JTBD N13) and a weaker sub-200 ms ride-through record than rotating-mass A1 incumbents (Piller, Hitec). Primary drivers: Safety 2 (fire envelope is a hard challenge), Reliability 5 (Li-ion trails flywheel on sub-200 ms proof). Market evidence: N08 ride-through proof opportunity=16 at satisfaction 4.

4-quadrant PCS delivers linearly more value with faster response and higher MW. Primary drivers: Reliability 7 (sub-100 ms PCS response enables N12 ≤16 ms transition), Cost 7 (revenue-stack unlocks N18 Netzentgelte+decarb). Not must_be — incumbents A1/A3 ride through without a 4-quadrant PCS. Market evidence: N12 opportunity=16.

MV coupling is must_be for grid-scale storage but overscoped for tool-level UPS incumbency (Schneider Galaxy, Piller at LV). Primary drivers: Reliability 7 (MV-side hardening maps to N03 MV feeder capacity), Safety 5 (proper grounding + protection relays). Delight 2 because fabs already own DSO-side interface.

EMS is the attractive delighter: incumbents Piller/Eaton/Vertiv are pure ride-through boxes with no revenue stack. Drivers: Cost 8 (unlocks N18 CFO scorecard, opp=13), Delight 7 (board-grade dual reporting is genuinely novel at this L5 slot). Absence would not dissatisfy — fabs historically buy ride-through without dispatch optimisation.

Highest overall_fit: maps directly to N05 certification delay (opp=17, the #1 ODI opportunity) and N06 parallel cert (opp=14). Drivers: Stress 8 + Time 8 (parallel-track certification competence), Skill 7 (EPC-led dossier discipline). Caveat — covers VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 only; SEMI F47 qualification (N08) remains a gap and must be added for fab-grade positioning.

must_be but weakest fit — duplicates fab GC civil scope (M+W, Exyte, Jacobs). Safety 6 from fire-clearance foundation detail. Market evidence: Mein Solar's civil capability is absorbed/ignored on S4 greenfield where the fab GC owns the yard.

step_10_product_related_needs.technology_class: Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC
step_10_product_related_needs.underlying_mechanism: Containerized Li-ion BESS + bidirectional PCS + MV grid coupling under single EPC contract
step_10_product_related_needs.commodity_functional_promise: Deliver operational grid-connected electrical energy storage assets as turnkey capital projects with single-source engineering, procurement, construction, and grid-code certification

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of a high-energy-density Li-ion thermal runaway event triggering >24 h fab-wide evacuation
needs.litmus_result: PASS — flywheel / DVR carry no Li-ion thermal runaway risk

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of fire-clearance zoning adding >15 m setback that forces BESS siting outside the fab security perimeter
needs.litmus_result: PASS — tool-level static UPS has no MV-BESS fire-clearance overhead

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of 10–15% round-trip energy losses eroding >15% of projected Netzentgelte peak-shaving and EPEX arbitrage revenue
needs.litmus_result: PASS — DVR is sub-cycle injection with no stored-energy RTE loss

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of ambient-extreme HVAC duty cycling reducing contracted ride-through autonomy by >5% within 24 months
needs.litmus_result: PASS — flywheel kinetic energy and DVR sub-cycle injection are not temperature-duty-cycle bound in the same way

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of LFP cell supply disruption (CN concentration, EU Battery Reg compliance) adding >12 weeks to fab-ramp-critical COD
needs.litmus_result: PASS — flywheel (EU-sourced steel/composite) and DVR (EU/US-sourced power electronics) have no LFP supply chain

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of installed cost exceeding €400/kWh such that CFO capex board rejects the project despite revenue-stack model
needs.litmus_result: PASS — €/kWh is a Li-ion-specific metric; flywheel/DVR have different cost unit (€/MW rating)

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of container footprint (40–80 m²/MWh) plus fire-service access requiring >20% of planned fab utility-yard area
needs.litmus_result: PASS — static UPS is rack-mounted tool-adjacent; DVR is cabinet-scale; neither consumes yard area at m²/MWh density

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack dispatch state of charge compromising the ≤16 ms source-transition window during a grid disturbance
needs.litmus_result: PASS — flywheel always at design-SoC for ride-through; dispatch-coupled SoC is a Li-ion-BESS-only conflict

Applies to any MV-connected storage EPC regardless of chemistry

strategic_read.entry_wedge: Lead with F5 (certification-path competence) + F4 (EMS revenue stack); mitigate F1 fire posture with engineered suppression and SEMI F47 qualification pathway
strategic_read.consistency_with_jtbd: High — P01↔N13, P04↔N14, P08↔N11+N12, P06↔N18 denominator all align
